place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Truly grateful for the great job done by Teacher Siva in taking care of my 2.5yo daughter on her first days of PN. She's patiently around to soothe her constant tears, ensuring she's fed when she gets too upset to eat, managed to soothe her to sleep and always tries to engage her in class activities. Hui Yi laoshi is also super patient and attentive to the needs/habits of my daughter, I can see an obvious effective partnership between Teacher Siva and her. They're the main reasons my anxiety has been greatly alleviated and I'm so much more assured now that my daughter will be well taken care of in the days to come.

I am also very thankful to Centre Director Josephine and Assistant Centre Director Liya for their steady presence and copious reassurance since our first contact until today. My husband and I never get a single negative feeling about the school each time we step in - it is always bright, clean, cheery and exudes a positive energy. The earnest and attentive teachers play a big part in creating this environment. Thank you Learning Vision NYP for being the Best Start in my daughter's growing up years!

Will recommend kids to enrol to LV @NYP

review_text:
Learning Vision @ NYP was my choice for my boys and frankly it is the best decision made. They played, learnt, cried and enjoyed school so much.

Special Appreciation to all the teachers and fellow staff

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: My child is currently studying at learning vision NYP. He used to be in infant care and just promoted to PG recently. I must say my son enjoys going to school and the team of teachers are really dedicated. Loved the fact that the teachers will update their progress and I enjoy looking at the photos. And also a big thumbs up to the Principal and v Principal who did a good job in handling the school! Also a special shout out to the infant care team of teachers especially teacher Aira who is very patient when it comes to answering my questions! 💪🏻

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
We are truly grateful to MindChamps Preschool @ Yio Chu Kang for grooming our son (now in P3) and giving him a strong foundation in both academics and character development. The school’s emphasis on building a champion mindset has positively shaped his confidence, resilience, and love for learning.

We also greatly appreciate the centre’s strong sports focus, which has allowed our son to develop an interest in swimming and ball sports, making him more active, confident, and athletic.

A heartfelt thank you to the dedicated teachers—especially 赵老师 and Ms Baey—for their patience, care, and commitment, as well as Mr Dave for his leadership and dedication in providing the best possible education for the children at MindChamps Yio Chu Kang. We highly recommend this preschool to parents who value holistic education and character development. Hope this review helps!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Our child first joined MindChamps when he was just 20 months old and spent about two wonderful years there before we had to switch to other preschools due to a residential move. After some time, we noticed that he wasn’t as well-prepared for Primary 1 as we had hoped, especially in Chinese, where he had almost no exposure. We decided to send him back to MindChamps in K2 at the Yio Chu Kang branch — and it turned out to be the best decision we could have made.

The teachers were incredibly patient and understanding. They guided our child gently even when he struggled to catch up with his peers, who were already quite advanced. There were times he felt discouraged and even cried out of stress, but thanks to the teachers’ care and encouragement, he gradually regained his confidence and began to enjoy learning again.

Now, just two months into Primary One, our child is thriving. He keeps up comfortably with the class curriculum and is confident with Hanyu Pinyin, needing little to no guidance. We’re very thankful for the MindChamps teaching model and the dedicated educators who made such a difference in his learning journey. We’re truly glad we made the choice to return.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: It has been a really pleasant and warmth experience with the school for my boys. Although my eldest has graduated, his teachers never fails to welcome him back whenever we pick our youngest from school ❤️

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
The river hongbao excursion is a worth trip for kids and parents. A lot of traditional costume and decoration related to CNY.
A great place to learn our traditions, thanks to all teachers for their explanation and efforts in the trip.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
We visited Maxwell Food Centre at around 8:00 AM on a Monday, so it was still relatively quiet with very few queues. Unfortunately, the famous chicken rice stall was closed, which was a bit disappointing. This was actually my second visit, and I still haven't had the chance to try it!

Thankfully, there are plenty of other stalls serving chicken rice, so we simply picked one without a line and it didn't disappoint. In fact, that's the beauty of Maxwell Food Centre. There are so many food options to choose from, and everything we tried was delicious. The drinks were great too, and the prices are very affordable. Those in the pictures are only some of those we found really delicious.

Whether you're craving local favorites or just looking for a good meal, Maxwell Food Centre never disappoints. If you're in Singapore, make sure to stop by for delicious food at budget-friendly prices. Just keep in mind that if you're hoping to try the famous chicken rice, it's worth checking the stall's opening hours before you go!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There’s a wide variety of food and the laksa was absolutely amazing. It’s cheaper if you pay cash instead of card. The staffs in charge of cleaning the trays were really proactive. We didn’t try the super crowded stalls, but the ones we had were pretty solid in terms of quality. Also no bad odor

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
I was here in the evening time and most of the stalls were closed for the day.
But I am very fortunate of finding a chicken rice shop still open.
Uncle Louis Famous chicken rice. Uncle was very hands on. He takes each customer's order attentively and gives suggestions along the way.
I ordered chicken with soup and veggies. Very tender, fresh veggie sides and hot rice. Yummy! Welcome local dinner on my 1st night in Singapore.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A step back in time with fantastic food variety at really bargain prices. Only one of the 3 stall rows has high velocity fans for extra cooling. A few obvious winners among the vendors. We had Vietnamese  Tofu and noodles and vegetarian rice paper rolls.
A local character has found a way to always get his favorite seat - Xtra load rap music. He managed to get a whole table under a fan for himself during the lunch peak. There's someone who understands marketing!
A fantastic authentic street food location that has a busy lunch peak but is much quieter otherwise.
The lady selling fresh crushed fruit and sugar can drinks is a standout nearthe China Town exit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: My favourite eating place in the city by far. So much to choose from, foodie heaven. There's always a good selection of shops open. Can be very busy at time with queues for the most popular places.  Don't be scared to share tables. When finished, put your tray in designated area before leaving.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Chomp Chomp was established in 1972 as a "hawkers centre", now better known as foodcentre. It has been upgraded a few times over the years, the latest one was end of 2025 till March 2026. Our cleaning manager gave us the tip about the great food at the very recently reopened foodhall. And she was spot on, what an absolute treat, this hidden gem. A must visit, higly recommended.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Favourite hawker centre !! I have been a regular diner at Chomp Chomp since I was young. The standard still feels around the same. I usually get the sambal stingray, oyster omelette, satay, bbq wings and carrot cake. The seats inside the centre are sometimes stuffy but the ones outside and at the back are really nice. love love love

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Lots of local dishes to choose. After renovation the place is still SMALL. Come early if you like a good seat. Better ventilation with the huge fans. Some of the stalls are no longer there.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
The XXL sugarcane juice is the hit.
Other foods are average, some of them require fairly long waiting time.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Closed from 27 October 2025 to 26 January 2026 for renovations and upgrading works. This is a great food centre with a diverse selection of hawker food. Been eating here when i was younger and living in Serangoon Gardens. My current fav char kway teow is located at this food centre and they have quite a few good stalls serving BBQ sambal stingray.

On my last visit I was surprised to see that Chomp Chomp isn't as crowded as it used to be back in the day. On the bright side, it’s way easier to get a seat now.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Weekdays is ok. Weekend is a fish market. Screaming infants and kids running around. Badly controlled during weekends due to small place. Avoid Sundays if you need a place to read.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I would like to commend the NEX Library for its attentiveness and highly proactive staff. It’s quite impressive how quickly they notice patrons—within just 30 minutes of me sitting on one of the couches, I was disturbed not once, but three times.

What stood out most was the level of dedication shown. One staff member even went the extra mile by physically slapping my chair to wake me up, followed by a firm reminder of “don’t sleep,” delivered publicly. It’s rare to see such enthusiasm in ensuring that no one gets too comfortable.

While I understand that seating is meant to be shared, I didn’t realise that briefly resting would warrant such persistent interruptions and physical gestures. It does make me wonder about the purpose of having couches in the library if taking a short rest isn’t acceptable.

Perhaps moving forward, the same level of attentiveness could be paired with a bit more courtesy and respect when approaching patrons. After all, maintaining a welcoming environment is just as important as enforcing rules.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: I really like the Serangoon library because there is a specific section where there are about 15 seats alone (near the entrance) And I think that's very helpful. There also is a children's section for 10-12 year olds and a 6-8 year old section right next to the 10-12 section. They also have comics for people that like to read comics. Close to the entrance. They have this display of books and I've picked a few books from the display that I've liked so that's very nice. There is a fairly large section for Fiction and I am a Fiction fan so that's great for me. There are multiple computers there too. I don't know if it's the best place to study as a student though. But the library's location is really nice because just one level down (after you exit the library) there's a mall. I personally really like the mall because there's a Fair Price Xtra, restaurants, gadget stores and more. I wish there were more isolated seats instead of many at a certain section. The location outside of the library and mall is also nice because there is a bus station and a taxi station. Overall I would say that the Serangoon library is a very nice library to visit.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Visited three times and harassed by staff each time. They have a children’s section but their expectations for toddler behavior are unrealistic. Zero empathy. I sent my family outside just to check out our books in peace. Even then I would still get nagged with unsolicited and irrelevant advice.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Overall experience is good just that at around 4pm onwards, the library will be super crowded. They have programme room which is free seating without the need to book table but from 1pm onwards, all tables are occupied. Should add another programme room. Also the library can be very cold too

review_text: ​This is a highly populated MRT station, which is expected given Ang Mo Kio's status as a major heartland hub. Despite the consistently heavy human traffic, especially during peak hours, it is extremely well-connected. The station offers fantastic convenience, with AMK Hub located right across the street, putting a vast array of amenities all within easy reach.

review_text:
Here’s a polished 100‑word Google Maps review tailored for Ang Mo Kio MRT Station (AMK):

---

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Ang Mo Kio MRT Station is one of the most convenient and well‑connected stations in Singapore. It links directly to the bus interchange and AMK Hub, making transfers smooth and efficient. The station is clean, well‑maintained, and equipped with lifts and escalators for accessibility. Signage is clear, so navigating between platforms and exits is easy even for first‑time visitors. Trains arrive on time, and staff are helpful when assistance is needed. Although it can get crowded during peak hours, the overall experience remains reliable. A dependable hub for daily commuting and shopping, right in the heart of Ang Mo Kio.

---

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Very cool multi level shop with mainly groceries but also some toys, gifts and collectables. Lots of interesting items, we spent a while looking at everything. The shop is spread out across multiple areas and levels with self serve checkouts. I didn't find it overly cheap like I was half expecting, thinking it was a discount store, but the prices werent overly expensive either.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: unique place, lots of history and charming layout. Definitely old school but well worth a visit in what is an ultra modern city in many ways

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: they hyped this up but no quality control. look at how dry it is. some parts is charred. not warm too. bought at 6pm with many more on the shelves thats not offering any heat.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: I was familiar with Don Quixote from a recent stay in Tokyo so I was looking forward to checking out a Singapore location while there for work. It didn't disappoint! It has all the normal goods on the upper level but the grocery store on the lower level was fantastic! The "deli" section had all kinds of bentos and sushi! I spent an hour just deciding what to take to the hotel to eat! If I return I will definitely be visiting this store every night for take home dinner!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: The best donki is singapore. More fresh food & more alcohol. 24 hrs with ample parking. Like a maze thou.
